question.text: Which of the following is a right of a mutual fund investor?

answer.text: Right to appoint the fund manager
Right to receive redemption proceeds within stipulated time and to file complaints with the AMC and SEBI
Right to decide which securities the fund should buy

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Key rights of mutual fund investors include: (1) Right to receive redemption proceeds within stipulated time (3 working days for equity, 1 for liquid funds), (2) Right to receive account statements, (3) Right to receive CAS, (4) Right to file complaints with the AMC and escalate to SEBI SCORES, (5) Right to receive scheme documents, (6) Right to appoint up to 3 nominees. **The Trap:** Investors do NOT have the right to guaranteed returns (mutual funds are market-linked), to appoint fund managers, or to decide individual security selections. These are the responsibilities of the AMC and fund manager. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Educate retired investors about their rights. Many investors are unaware of their right to timely redemption, regular statements, and the complaint escalation process. Empowered investors make better investment decisions.
